description: Authoritative state of known ME/CFS drug trials in Germany. Updated by the routine in ROUTINE.md. The first run after setup will (correctly) flag every relevant trial it finds as 'new', because this baseline starts empty. Do not hand-fabricate entries — only add trials confirmed from a registry or institutional source during a check.

trials.name: Observational Study on Immunoadsorption (IA) in Patients With Autoantibody-Positive Post-Infectious ME/CFS

intervention.name: Immunoadsorption (TheraSorb®)
intervention.mechanism: Removes GPCR autoantibodies (adrenergic, antineuronal) from blood via TheraSorb® affinity column (Miltenyi); 5 outpatient sessions over 2 weeks.

trials.sponsor: Charité University, Berlin (supported by German Federal Ministry of Research and Weidenhammer-Zöbele Foundation)

sites.institution: Charité - Universitätsmedizin Berlin, Institute for Medical Immunology

eligibility.summary: Adults 18–65 with post-infectious ME/CFS (Canadian Consensus Criteria), Bell Disability Score <60, post-exertional malaise ≥14 h, and detectable autoantibodies (adrenergic or antineuronal).
key_inclusion: ME/CFS per Canadian Consensus Criteria with PEM ≥14 h
key_exclusion: Conditions that preclude definite ME/CFS diagnosis

trials.notes: Immunoadsorption is apheresis (borderline for drug/pharmacological criterion) — included per ROUTINE.md: 'immunoadsorption is borderline → include + note it'; is_drug_study set to false. Status verified recruiting on ClinicalTrials.gov (April 2026). Contact: Dr. Elisa A Stein, elisa.stein@charite.de, +49 450 624354. Successor to the earlier Trial RIA (NCT05629988).

trials.name: MDC002 Phase 1 Clinical Trial in ME/CFS (planned)

intervention.name: MDC002
intervention.mechanism: Orally applicable small molecule; stimulates Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase and mitochondrial Na⁺/Ca²⁺ exchanger NCLX in skeletal muscle to interrupt calcium-overload cycle and restore cellular energy production.

trials.condition: ME/CFS, post-acute infection syndromes with exertional intolerance / PEM
trials.phase: Phase 1 (planned; pre-clinical stage as of mid-2026)

eligibility.summary: To be defined when trial opens; clinical trials are in planning stage as of June 2026.

trials.notes: Not yet registered in any trial registry. German criterion satisfied by German sponsor (Mitodicure GmbH). Pipeline page (mitodicure.com/pipeline/) confirms MDC002 is 'ready for GLP toxicity and GLP safety pharmacology studies' as of June 2026 — i.e. still pre-clinical; no Phase 1 registration or new funding announcement. Watch for Phase 1 registration on DRKS or ClinicalTrials.gov.
